# UC-14.2.1 · PLC/RTU Health Monitoring

## Description

Controller failures halt industrial processes. Monitoring CPU, memory, and communication status prevents unplanned downtime.

## Value

Controller failures halt industrial processes. Monitoring CPU, memory, and communication status prevents unplanned downtime.

## Implementation

Connect to PLCs via OPC-UA server or Modbus gateway through Splunk Edge Hub. Poll health metrics every 30 seconds. Alert on CPU >80%, memory >90%, or communication loss. Track uptime per controller.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OPC-UA input, Modbus TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: OPC-UA metrics (CPU, memory, I/O status), Modbus register data.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Connect to PLCs via OPC-UA server or Modbus gateway through Splunk Edge Hub. Poll health metrics every 30 seconds. Alert on CPU >80%, memory >90%, or communication loss. Track uptime per controller.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:metrics"
| where plc_cpu_pct > 80 OR plc_memory_pct > 90 OR comm_status!="OK"
| table _time, plc_name, plc_cpu_pct, plc_memory_pct, comm_status
```

Understanding this SPL

**PLC/RTU Health Monitoring** — Controller failures halt industrial processes. Monitoring CPU, memory, and communication status prevents unplanned downtime.

Documented **Data sources**: OPC-UA metrics (CPU, memory, I/O status), Modbus register data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OPC-UA input, Modbus TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: opcua:metrics. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="opcua:metrics". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where plc_cpu_pct > 80 OR plc_memory_pct > 90 OR comm_status!="OK"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **PLC/RTU Health Monitoring**): table _time, plc_name, plc_cpu_pct, plc_memory_pct, comm_status


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (PLC × health), Gauge (CPU/memory per PLC), Line chart (health trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:metrics"
| where plc_cpu_pct > 80 OR plc_memory_pct > 90 OR comm_status!="OK"
| table _time, plc_name, plc_cpu_pct, plc_memory_pct, comm_status
```

## Visualization

Status grid (PLC × health), Gauge (CPU/memory per PLC), Line chart (health trend).
